mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 7.0% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions that drive equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 60d returns decline despite concurrent growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly core inflation reading (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank policy tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity that depresses equity earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 99.0.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3.0% indicates strong economic expansion that drives corporate profits higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 60%+ of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) above 200k combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 7.0% signals a strong labor market that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 20d returns decline despite both metrics meeting thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in core inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows positive coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions, or 5d returns rise despite both metrics exceeding thresholds.
